source: ratified frozen briefing (evals/replay/leak-devon/expected.json) — seeded offline, no live call

prose.headline: Devon is aiming at tech lead, and the session surfaced a real stretch path through cross-team scope and delegation.
summary_bullets: He keeps translating growth into concrete scope — mentoring juniors, owning harder system work, and a stretch project into another team’s area.
The gap is not ambition; it is exposure and tradeoff clarity, since he asked for more visibility and more architecture-review presence without naming what gets dropped.
prose.understanding_paragraph: Devon is not vague about direction: he wants tech lead within a year and is already thinking in terms of delegation. What we did not learn is whether that path is backed by a specific project, stakeholder exposure, or scope change that will prove the next step.
prose.brutal_truth_employee: Devon is not drifting in the abstract; he said "Wants to grow into a tech lead role over the next year" and kept pointing to bigger scope. The weak spot is that his growth story still stops at aspiration unless he gets a named project or stakeholder exposure that proves it.
prose.brutal_truth_manager: You got the growth thread moving, but the moment to press was when he said "Wants to present more often in the architecture review" — that was a request for authority without a concrete decision or scope. Next time, pin him to one project or stakeholder so the conversation ends with ownership, not wish-listing.

next_actions.action: Assign Devon one cross-team project with a named stakeholder and clear decision rights.
Ask Devon to name the one scope change that would prove tech lead readiness this quarter.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: see whether Devon has taken the architecture review slot or only talked about it.
Within two weeks: if the stretch project stays vague, treat the tech lead plan as aspiration, not momentum.
